storyline:
MP Materials (MP) is the main US rare-earth miner. It rose on China's rare-earth export curbs — Beijing restricting critical-mineral exports makes a domestic supplier strategically valuable. The catalyst is open-ended (ongoing trade tension, no single resolution date); MP's price flattened around Nov 2025 as the scarcity premium faded. What we'd want: exit when the curbs ease / the premium fades (~Nov 2025). Here the agent held the position live the whole window — the under-exit case, because an open-ended catalyst never cleanly 'resolves' on a date.
Co-discovered events the curator juggled (not this dashboard's gem):
  • ev1 · HNAOF — catalyst: Trump alliance rupture spurs global defense spending boom. Exit: still live — none - no NATO reaffirmation, peace settlement, or defense-budget cuts have reversed allied self-arm driver.
  • ev3 · SATS — catalyst: SpaceX buys EchoStar spectrum licenses for $17B deal. Exit: still live — No reversal; deal still pending FCC approval, but 15 weeks of total silence suggests thesis has lost all market relevance.
  • ev4 · RKLB — catalyst: Rocket Lab hits record high on new JAXA launch deal. Exit: ✕ EXITED 2025-10-24 — JAXA contract is a single disclosed deal already public and priced at record high; no reversal of driving condition applies (acute catalyst).
  • ev5 · DNN — catalyst: Denison Mines signs landmark uranium development pact with Athabasca communities. Exit: ✕ EXITED 2025-12-05 — The pact was already signed/announced on Dec 1, before entry — the named deal has closed and is now public/priced.
